digest.component_or_area: MCP auth framework (composite audience choke point + resource-server wiring + static bearer verifier)
propositions: Add flat scalar auth config keys (auth_enabled, auth_strategies, auth_issuer_url, auth_resource_server_url, auth_required_scopes, auth_static_tokens_file) to McpConfig with coercer entries, manifest registration, and regenerated reference docs.

Implement CompositeTokenVerifier as the single fail-closed choke point that tries active verifiers in order, treats None as skip and raises as non-acceptance, and rejects any candidate token whose resource is absent or not equal to auth_resource_server_url.

Build a config→verifier factory over the closed strategy set (static|jwt|introspection|proxy|custom) that hard-fails startup on unknown strategy, empty composite when auth enabled, or auth over HTTP with no verifier wired.

Implement StaticBearerVerifier that parses a secrets file, stores only SHA-256 digests, compares with constant-time hmac, assigns client_id/scopes, and stamps resource=auth_resource_server_url; reject records with plaintext literals or missing both token_env and token_sha256.

Wire auth into build_server so FastMCP serves the RFC 9728 PRM with a single authorization_servers entry and emits 401 + WWW-Authenticate, resolving the unauthenticated-HTTP guard against real auth_enabled.
Add a redaction helper on all auth error/log paths so no token or secret substring is ever emitted; auth remains additive and off by default.
